data_json.content: The configs are identical and only register `feedback-mcp` from the requested set; the other five are genuinely absent. The discriminating check for `feedback-mcp` is now startup behavior: if `cargo run -p feedback-mcp` builds but emits protocol-breaking stdout or resolves the wrong working directory, Copilot will hide/fail it even though the JSON entry exists.

arguments.title: [mcp-config] Register and activate local agent MCP servers in VS Code Copilot
arguments.description:
## Goal
Make feedback-mcp, session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp discoverable and usable from GitHub Copilot Chat in this VS Code workspace.

## Scope
- Diagnose why feedback-mcp is configured but not listed.
- Add missing local stdio server registrations to both workspace MCP configuration surfaces.
- Validate JSON syntax, Cargo package resolution, and an MCP initialize/tools-list handshake for all six servers.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both `.vscode/mcp.json` and `.github/mcp.json` contain consistent registrations for all six servers.
- Each configured command starts from the workspace and completes MCP initialization without protocol errors.
- Validation evidence and any required VS Code reload/restart step are documented.

data_json.content: The local hypothesis is now concrete: five entries were never added, while `feedback-mcp` is configured but likely failed during discovery/startup. I’m recording that requirement, claiming the two config files, then I’ll validate all six binaries through an MCP initialization handshake rather than merely checking that Cargo can compile them.

arguments.intent: Diagnose and activate six local MCP servers for VS Code Copilot Chat

arguments.title: VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration
arguments.body:
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Requirement
The workspace MCP configuration must expose the repository's agent-facing stdio servers through both VS Code and GitHub Copilot configuration surfaces.

The required local servers are `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp`.

## Behavior
- `.vscode/mcp.json` and `.github/mcp.json` must carry equivalent server definitions.
- Each server launches from the repository Cargo workspace using its package name.
- Configuration must use the `servers` object consumed by VS Code; the compatibility `mcpServers` object remains synchronized while clients still consume it.
- Server activation is validated with MCP initialization and tool enumeration, not only compilation.

## Traceability
Related ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.

# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Requirement
The workspace MCP configuration must expose the repository's agent-facing stdio servers through both VS Code and GitHub Copilot configuration surfaces.

The required local servers are `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp`.

## Behavior
- `.vscode/mcp.json` and `.github/mcp.json` must carry equivalent server definitions.
- Each server launches from the repository Cargo workspace using its package name.
- Configuration must use the `servers` object consumed by VS Code; the compatibility `mcpServers` object remains synchronized while clients still consume it.
- Server activation is validated with MCP initialization and tool enumeration, not only compilation.

Related ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.

data_json.content: The entrypoints write diagnostics to stderr, so they do not corrupt stdio JSON-RPC. The evidence points to two configuration causes: five servers are absent, and `feedback-mcp` was present but this Copilot session’s server/tool snapshot was created before it was activated or successfully enumerated; a window reload is required after changing MCP registrations. I’m adding the five missing packages consistently to both accepted schema objects in both config files.

arguments.command: rtk node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const files=['.vscode/mcp.json','.github/mcp.json']; const parsed=files.map(file=>JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(file,'utf8'))); for (const [index,config] of parsed.entries()) { if (JSON.stringify(config.servers)!==JSON.stringify(config.mcpServers)) throw new Error(files[index]+' schema maps differ'); } if (JSON.stringify(parsed[0])!==JSON.stringify(parsed[1])) throw new Error('MCP config files differ'); const required=['feedback-mcp','session-mcp','peek-mcp','rule-mcp','audit-mcp','compact-terminal-mcp']; for (const name of required) if (!parsed[0].servers[name]) throw new Error('missing '+name); console.log('validated '+required.length+' required MCP registrations in both synchronized configs');"
arguments.explanation: Parse both MCP JSON files and verify all six required servers plus synchronized schema maps.

data_json.content: The registrations are in place. The immediate focused check is JSON validity plus exact equality between the `servers` and `mcpServers` maps across both files; if that passes, I’ll build and handshake the six configured commands.

arguments.command: rtk node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const files=[".vscode/mcp.json",".github/mcp.json"]; const parsed=files.map(file=>JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(file,"utf8"))); for (const [index,config] of parsed.entries()) { if (JSON.stringify(config.servers) !== JSON.stringify(config.mcpServers)) throw new Error(files[index]+" schema maps differ"); } if (JSON.stringify(parsed[0]) !== JSON.stringify(parsed[1])) throw new Error("MCP config files differ"); const required=["feedback-mcp","session-mcp","peek-mcp","rule-mcp","audit-mcp","compact-terminal-mcp"]; for (const name of required) { if (parsed[0].servers[name] === undefined) throw new Error("missing "+name); } console.log("validated "+required.length+" required MCP registrations in both synchronized configs");'
arguments.explanation: Rerun the MCP JSON consistency check with shell-safe quoting.

data_json.content: The first check hit Bash history expansion on JavaScript’s `!` operator before Node ran; the config itself was not evaluated. I’m rerunning the same check without shell-sensitive syntax, then proceeding directly to package builds if it passes.

arguments.command: MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' tasklist.exe /FI 'IMAGENAME eq feedback-mcp.exe' /FO CSV
arguments.explanation: Identify the Windows process locking the feedback MCP release binary.

data_json.content: The release build exposed a real activation problem: `feedback-mcp.exe` is already running and Windows has locked the binary, so Cargo cannot refresh it. I’m identifying the owning process before deciding whether it is a healthy VS Code MCP instance to leave alone or a stale process to stop; the other five packages reached the build queue without source errors.

data_json.content: There is one live `feedback-mcp.exe` process, which explains both the lock and why blindly rebuilding it would be disruptive. I’m checking its parent and command line, while separately building the five newly registered servers; this keeps the active feedback instance intact unless it proves stale.

arguments.command: MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' wmic.exe process where processid=12412 get ProcessId,ParentProcessId,CommandLine /format:list
arguments.explanation: Inspect the feedback MCP process parent and launch command to determine whether VS Code owns it.

arguments.command: ps -W -p 12412 -o ppid,pid,args
arguments.explanation: Use the available process viewer to identify the live feedback MCP process and its parent.

data_json.content: The five new servers build cleanly in release mode. `wmic` is unavailable on this Windows installation, so I’m using the existing Bash process view to identify the feedback parent, then I’ll run protocol handshakes against all six binaries without rebuilding the locked one.

arguments.command:
rtk node -e 'const {spawn}=require("child_process"); const names=["feedback-mcp","session-mcp","peek-mcp","rule-mcp","audit-mcp","compact-terminal-mcp"]; async function test(name){ const child=spawn(`target/release/${name}.exe`,[],{cwd:process.cwd(),stdio:["pipe","pipe","pipe"]}); let buffer="",stderr=""; const waits=new Map(); child.stderr.on("data",data=>stderr+=data); child.stdout.on("data",data=>{ buffer+=data; let newline; while((newline=buffer.indexOf("
"))>=0){ const line=buffer.slice(0,newline).trim(); buffer=buffer.slice(newline+1); if(!line) continue; let message; try{message=JSON.parse(line)}catch(error){continue} if(message.id!==undefined && waits.has(message.id)){ waits.get(message.id)(message); waits.delete(message.id); } } }); const call=(id,method,params)=>new Promise((resolve,reject)=>{ const timer=setTimeout(()=>{waits.delete(id);reject(new Error(`${name} timed out on ${method}; stderr=${stderr}`))},30000); waits.set(id,message=>{clearTimeout(timer); if(message.error) reject(new Error(`${name} ${method}: ${JSON.stringify(message.error)}`)); else resolve(message.result)}); child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",id,method,params})+"
"); }); try { const initialized=await call(1,"initialize",{protocolVersion:"2024-11-05",capabilities:{},clientInfo:{name:"mcp-config-validation",version:"1.0.0"}}); child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",method:"notifications/initialized"})+"
"); const listed=await call(2,"tools/list",{}); const tools=(listed.tools||[]).map(tool=>tool.name); console.log(`${name}: initialized ${initialized.serverInfo?.name||"server"}; tools=${tools.length} [${tools.join(", ")}]`); } finally { child.kill(); } } (async()=>{for(const name of names) await test(name)})().catch(error=>{console.error(error.message);process.exit(1)});'
arguments.explanation: Launch each configured binary and validate MCP initialize plus tools/list responses over stdio.

data_json.content: All six executables are now available, including the already-built feedback binary. I’m launching fresh instances one at a time and requiring both MCP initialization and tool enumeration; this will catch protocol, store-resolution, or startup failures that compilation alone cannot.

arguments.command: rtk git diff -- .vscode/mcp.json .github/mcp.json
arguments.explanation: Review only the two MCP configuration changes for accidental drift.

data_json.content: The protocol check passes for every server, with 56 tools enumerated across the six processes. I’m doing the final editor-facing validation now: JSON/schema diagnostics and a focused diff, then I’ll update the ticket/spec with the exact cause, validation evidence, and the one unavoidable activation step, reloading the VS Code window so Copilot rebuilds its MCP tool snapshot.

data_json.content: The editor schema check found the deeper reason: `.vscode/mcp.json` contains a top-level `mcpServers` property that VS Code explicitly rejects. That can invalidate discovery even when individual entries are correct. I’m removing that compatibility object from the VS Code file only, while retaining it in `.github/mcp.json`, whose schema accepts it; then I’ll rerun schema and protocol checks against the corrected client-specific shapes.

arguments.command: rtk node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const vscode=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".vscode/mcp.json","utf8")); const github=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".github/mcp.json","utf8")); const required=["feedback-mcp","session-mcp","peek-mcp","rule-mcp","audit-mcp","compact-terminal-mcp"]; if (vscode.mcpServers !== undefined) throw new Error("VS Code config contains unsupported mcpServers"); if (JSON.stringify(github.servers) !== JSON.stringify(github.mcpServers)) throw new Error("GitHub schema maps differ"); if (JSON.stringify(vscode.servers) !== JSON.stringify(github.servers)) throw new Error("client server definitions differ"); for (const name of required) { if (vscode.servers[name] === undefined) throw new Error("missing "+name); } console.log("validated client-specific schemas and "+required.length+" synchronized MCP registrations");'

arguments.body:
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Requirement
The workspace MCP configuration must expose the repository's agent-facing stdio servers to GitHub Copilot Chat through VS Code's supported MCP schema.

The required local servers are `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp`.

## Behavior
- `.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`; the target is the single source of truth for both paths.
- The shared target uses the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema.
- The shared target must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object, because VS Code rejects that property and may fail server discovery.
- Each server launches from the repository Cargo workspace using its package name.
- Server activation is validated with MCP initialization and tool enumeration, not only compilation.
- A VS Code window reload is required after registration changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

## Traceability
Related ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.

Validation on 2026-07-15: both linked paths pass editor schema diagnostics; all six binaries completed MCP `initialize` and `tools/list`, enumerating 56 tools in total.

arguments.command: rtk node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const vscode=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".vscode/mcp.json","utf8")); const github=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(".github/mcp.json","utf8")); const required=["feedback-mcp","session-mcp","peek-mcp","rule-mcp","audit-mcp","compact-terminal-mcp"]; for (const config of [vscode,github]) { if (config.mcpServers !== undefined) throw new Error("unsupported mcpServers property"); for (const name of required) { if (config.servers[name] === undefined) throw new Error("missing "+name); } } if (JSON.stringify(vscode) !== JSON.stringify(github)) throw new Error("symlink views differ"); console.log("validated shared servers-only schema and "+required.length+" required registrations");'
arguments.explanation: Validate both symlink views use the supported servers-only schema and contain all required registrations.

arguments.description:
## Goal
Make feedback-mcp, session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp discoverable and usable from GitHub Copilot Chat in this VS Code workspace.

## Root cause
- Five requested servers were absent from the shared MCP registration.
- `.vscode/mcp.json` is a symlink to `.github/mcp.json`.
- The shared target contained a top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object that the VS Code MCP schema rejects. This could invalidate discovery, including the already-present feedback-mcp entry.
- Copilot snapshots MCP tools for a chat/window; a window reload is required after registration changes.

## Implementation
- Added session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp to the supported `servers` map.
- Removed the unsupported `mcpServers` object from the shared target.
- Preserved feedback-mcp and existing context/ticket/spec/test/log-viewer registrations.

## Validation
- Editor diagnostics: no errors for either linked MCP config path.
- Release build: five newly registered packages passed; feedback-mcp was already active and Windows-locked.
- Protocol: all six fresh processes completed MCP `initialize` and `tools/list`, enumerating 56 tools total.
- Validation spec: `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`.
- Passing execution: `exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration-20260715`.
- Requirement spec: `.spec/specs/1a62c7f7-4f94-420b-9532-3a28b0c1ecd5/spec.toml`.

## Activation
Run `Developer: Reload Window` in VS Code. Copilot will then rebuild the MCP server list and expose the newly registered tools to subsequent agent prompts.
arguments.author: GitHub Copilot

data_json.content: Durable validation evidence is recorded as passed. I’m finalizing traceability now by adding the spec and execution IDs to the ticket, estimating the work, advancing it to review, and checking the spec lifecycle rules before changing its state.

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Motivation
Agent prompts need direct access to the repository's feedback, session, inspection, rule, audit, and compact terminal capabilities. Invalid or incomplete workspace MCP registration prevents GitHub Copilot Chat from discovering those local stdio servers.

## Dependent expectation
If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on GitHub Copilot Chat discovering `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp` after a VS Code window reload, with each server able to initialize and enumerate its tools over stdio.

`.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`, making the target the single source of truth. The shared target uses only the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema. It must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object.

## Guards
- `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`: requires editor schema validation, all six registrations, release package availability, and successful MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` responses.
- Passing evidence: `exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration-20260715` enumerated 56 tools across the six servers.

## Positions
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .github/mcp.json`: canonical `servers` registrations for the six local MCP packages.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .vscode/mcp.json`: VS Code-facing symlink to the canonical configuration.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/main.rs`: feedback stdio server startup.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/main.rs`: session stdio server startup.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/peek-mcp/src/main.rs`: peek stdio server startup.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/main.rs`: rule stdio server startup.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/audit-mcp/src/main.rs`: audit stdio server startup.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/compact-terminal-mcp/src/main.rs`: compact terminal stdio server startup.

## Governing-rule requirement
The session bootstrap policy at `.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/body.md` must introduce task-relevant workspace guidance, including this MCP availability contract when agent tooling configuration is changed.

## Traceability
- Ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.
- Validation spec: `.test/default/specs/val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration.json`.
- Passing execution: `.test/default/executions/exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration-20260715.json`.

A VS Code window reload is required after registration changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

data_json.content:
**Root cause**

- Five servers were missing from the configuration.
- [.vscode/mcp.json](.vscode/mcp.json) is a symlink to [.github/mcp.json](.github/mcp.json).
- The shared file contained an unsupported top-level `mcpServers` property. VS Code reported a schema error, which could prevent discovery of the already-configured `feedback-mcp`.

The shared configuration now uses the supported `servers` property and registers:

`feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp`.

All six completed MCP `initialize` and `tools/list`; together they exposed 56 tools. Both configuration paths now pass editor diagnostics.

Run **Developer: Reload Window** from the VS Code command palette. Copilot will rebuild its MCP server snapshot, after which the six servers should appear for new chats and agent prompts.

data_json.content: The API confirms the cause exactly: `Default::default()` calls `Implementation::from_build_env()` inside the dependency crate, so every server reports `rmcp` rather than the consuming package. I’ll explicitly populate `server_info` with each package name/version; for feedback, I’m also checking process multiplicity and current config visibility because a stale pre-fix process can remain absent or mislabeled until it is restarted.

arguments.title: [mcp] Report unique server identities for local Copilot registrations
arguments.description:
## Goal
Make feedback-mcp, session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp report their true package identities in MCP initialize responses so VS Code Copilot can distinguish and activate every configured server.

## Root cause hypothesis
Each ServerHandler builds ServerInfo with `..Default::default()`. rmcp's default Implementation is compiled inside the rmcp crate and therefore reports name `rmcp` for every consumer. Duplicate initialize identities can collapse or obscure configured registrations; feedback-mcp is configured but is not launched or shown.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each of the six servers returns its package name and package version in `serverInfo`.
- Fresh MCP initialize responses have six unique names.
- feedback-mcp starts and tools/list includes all feedback tools.
- Focused package tests and release handshake validation pass.

arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Motivation
Agent prompts need direct access to the repository's feedback, session, inspection, rule, audit, and compact terminal capabilities. Invalid, incomplete, or identity-colliding workspace MCP registrations prevent GitHub Copilot Chat from discovering those local stdio servers.

## Dependent expectation
If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on GitHub Copilot Chat discovering `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp` after a VS Code window reload. Each server returns its true package name and version in the MCP initialize `serverInfo`, and can enumerate its tools over stdio.

`.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`, making the target the single source of truth. The shared target uses only the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema. It must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object.

## Guards
- `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`: requires editor schema validation, all six registrations, six unique package-level initialize names, and successful MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` responses.
- Passing evidence must include a live `feedback-mcp` initialize response and its feedback tool list.

## Positions
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .github/mcp.json`: canonical `servers` registrations for the six local MCP packages.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .vscode/mcp.json`: VS Code-facing symlink to the canonical configuration.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs`: feedback tools work, but initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/peek-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/audit-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/compact-terminal-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.

## Governing-rule requirement
The session bootstrap policy at `.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/body.md` must introduce task-relevant workspace guidance, including this MCP availability contract when agent tooling configuration is changed.

## Traceability
- Configuration ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.
- Identity remediation ticket: `memory-api/.ticket/tickets/<resolved-by-ticket-api>/ticket.toml`.
- Validation spec: `.test/default/specs/val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration.json`.

A VS Code window reload is required after registration or server identity changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

data_json.content: Feedback is configured correctly but no `feedback-mcp.exe` process exists, and Copilot’s current debug logs contain no launch attempt or failure. That means it is being lost during discovery before process startup, consistent with the duplicate `rmcp` identity collision: six registrations initialize under the same server name, and feedback is one of the colliding entries. I’m creating a focused memory-api ticket, updating the existing MCP contract, and claiming the six handlers before editing.

Make feedback-mcp, session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp report their true package identities in MCP initialize responses so VS Code Copilot can distinguish and activate every configured server.

## Root cause hypothesis
Each ServerHandler builds ServerInfo with `..Default::default()`. rmcp's default Implementation is compiled inside the rmcp crate and therefore reports name `rmcp` for every consumer. Duplicate initialize identities can collapse or obscure configured registrations; feedback-mcp is configured but is not launched or shown.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each of the six servers returns its package name and package version in `serverInfo`.
- Fresh MCP initialize responses have six unique names.
- feedback-mcp starts and tools/list includes all feedback tools.
- Focused package tests and release handshake validation pass.

tool_arguments_json.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Motivation
Agent prompts need direct access to the repository's feedback, session, inspection, rule, audit, and compact terminal capabilities. Invalid, incomplete, or identity-colliding workspace MCP registrations prevent GitHub Copilot Chat from discovering those local stdio servers.

## Dependent expectation
If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on GitHub Copilot Chat discovering `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp` after a VS Code window reload. Each server returns its true package name and version in the MCP initialize `serverInfo`, and can enumerate its tools over stdio.

`.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`, making the target the single source of truth. The shared target uses only the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema. It must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object.

## Guards
- `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`: requires editor schema validation, all six registrations, six unique package-level initialize names, and successful MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` responses.
- Passing evidence must include a live `feedback-mcp` initialize response and its feedback tool list.

## Positions
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .github/mcp.json`: canonical `servers` registrations for the six local MCP packages.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .vscode/mcp.json`: VS Code-facing symlink to the canonical configuration.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs`: feedback tools work, but initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/peek-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/audit-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/compact-terminal-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.

## Governing-rule requirement
The session bootstrap policy at `.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/body.md` must introduce task-relevant workspace guidance, including this MCP availability contract when agent tooling configuration is changed.

## Traceability
- Configuration ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.
- Identity remediation ticket: `memory-api/.ticket/tickets/<resolved-by-ticket-api>/ticket.toml`.
- Validation spec: `.test/default/specs/val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration.json`.

A VS Code window reload is required after registration or server identity changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Motivation
Agent prompts need direct access to the repository's feedback, session, inspection, rule, audit, and compact terminal capabilities. Invalid, incomplete, or identity-colliding workspace MCP registrations prevent GitHub Copilot Chat from discovering those local stdio servers.

## Dependent expectation
If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on GitHub Copilot Chat discovering `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp` after a VS Code window reload. Each server returns its true package name and version in the MCP initialize `serverInfo`, and can enumerate its tools over stdio.

`.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`, making the target the single source of truth. The shared target uses only the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema. It must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object.

## Guards
- `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`: requires editor schema validation, all six registrations, six unique package-level initialize names, and successful MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` responses.
- Passing evidence must include a live `feedback-mcp` initialize response and its feedback tool list.

## Positions
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .github/mcp.json`: canonical `servers` registrations for the six local MCP packages.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .vscode/mcp.json`: VS Code-facing symlink to the canonical configuration.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs`: feedback tools work, but initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/peek-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/audit-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.
- `partial` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/compact-terminal-mcp/src/server.rs`: initialize metadata currently inherits the colliding `rmcp` identity.

## Governing-rule requirement
The session bootstrap policy at `.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/body.md` must introduce task-relevant workspace guidance, including this MCP availability contract when agent tooling configuration is changed.

## Traceability
- Configuration ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.
- Identity remediation ticket: `memory-api/.ticket/tickets/2318f63f-7113-4987-87b7-ca26afa04d11/ticket.toml`.
- Validation spec: `.test/default/specs/val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration.json`.

A VS Code window reload is required after registration or server identity changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

arguments.command: MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' tasklist.exe /FO CSV | grep -E '"(feedback-mcp|session-mcp|peek-mcp|rule-mcp|audit-mcp|compact-terminal-mcp)\.exe"'
arguments.explanation: List the currently running MCP executables that are locking stale release binaries.

data_json.content: The release build is blocked because VS Code currently has the five visible MCP executables running and Windows locks them; feedback is notably not among the locked processes, confirming it was never activated. To apply the identity fix, I need to stop those stale MCP processes, rebuild all six, and validate fresh instances. VS Code will restart them on reload.

arguments.command: MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' taskkill.exe /F /IM peek-mcp.exe /IM compact-terminal-mcp.exe /IM rule-mcp.exe /IM audit-mcp.exe /IM session-mcp.exe
arguments.explanation: Stop the five stale MCP processes so their corrected release binaries can be rebuilt.

data_json.content: The process list confirms exactly five stale visible servers and no feedback process. I’m terminating those five stale instances now, rebuilding all six release executables, then immediately handshaking the fresh binaries before VS Code gets a chance to cache the old identity again.

arguments.command:
rtk node -e 'const {spawn}=require("child_process"); const names=["feedback-mcp","session-mcp","peek-mcp","rule-mcp","audit-mcp","compact-terminal-mcp"]; async function test(name){ const child=spawn(`target/release/${name}.exe`,[],{cwd:process.cwd(),stdio:["pipe","pipe","pipe"]}); let buffer="",stderr=""; const waits=new Map(); child.stderr.on("data",data=>stderr+=data); child.stdout.on("data",data=>{ buffer+=data; let newline; while((newline=buffer.indexOf("
"))>=0){ const line=buffer.slice(0,newline).trim(); buffer=buffer.slice(newline+1); if(!line) continue; let message; try{message=JSON.parse(line)}catch{continue} if(message.id!==undefined&&waits.has(message.id)){waits.get(message.id)(message);waits.delete(message.id)} } }); const call=(id,method,params)=>new Promise((resolve,reject)=>{const timer=setTimeout(()=>{waits.delete(id);reject(new Error(`${name} timed out on ${method}; stderr=${stderr}`))},30000);waits.set(id,message=>{clearTimeout(timer);message.error?reject(new Error(`${name} ${method}: ${JSON.stringify(message.error)}`)):resolve(message.result)});child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",id,method,params})+"
")}); try{const initialized=await call(1,"initialize",{protocolVersion:"2024-11-05",capabilities:{},clientInfo:{name:"identity-validation",version:"1.0.0"}});child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",method:"notifications/initialized"})+"
");const listed=await call(2,"tools/list",{});const actual=initialized.serverInfo?.name;if(actual!==name)throw new Error(`${name} reported ${actual}`);const tools=(listed.tools||[]).map(tool=>tool.name);if(name==="feedback-mcp"&&tools.length!==5)throw new Error(`feedback-mcp exposed ${tools.length} tools`);console.log(`${actual}@${initialized.serverInfo.version}: ${tools.length} tools [${tools.join(", ")}]`);return actual}finally{child.kill()}} (async()=>{const actual=[];for(const name of names)actual.push(await test(name));if(new Set(actual).size!==names.length)throw new Error("initialize names are not unique");console.log(`validated ${actual.length} unique MCP server identities`)} )().catch(error=>{console.error(error.message);process.exit(1)});'
arguments.explanation: Require six unique package-level initialize names and verify feedback-mcp exposes all five feedback tools.

data_json.content: The corrected release binaries now build successfully. I’m running the strict initialize/tools-list harness; this is the decisive check for both the display-name fix and feedback availability.

"))>=0){ const line=buffer.slice(0,newline).trim(); buffer=buffer.slice(newline+1); if(!line) continue; let message; try{message=JSON.parse(line)}catch{continue} if(message.id!==undefined&&waits.has(message.id)){waits.get(message.id)(message);waits.delete(message.id)} } }); const call=(id,method,params)=>new Promise((resolve,reject)=>{const timer=setTimeout(()=>{waits.delete(id);reject(new Error(`${name} timed out on ${method}; stderr=${stderr}`))},30000);waits.set(id,message=>{clearTimeout(timer);message.error?reject(new Error(`${name} ${method}: ${JSON.stringify(message.error)}`)):resolve(message.result)});child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",id,method,params})+"
")}); try{const initialized=await call(1,"initialize",{protocolVersion:"2024-11-05",capabilities:{},clientInfo:{name:"identity-validation",version:"1.0.0"}});child.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({jsonrpc:"2.0",method:"notifications/initialized"})+"
");const listed=await call(2,"tools/list",{});const actual=initialized.serverInfo?.name;if(actual!==name)throw new Error(`${name} reported ${actual}`);const tools=(listed.tools||[]).map(tool=>tool.name);if(name==="feedback-mcp"&&tools.length!==5)throw new Error(`feedback-mcp exposed ${tools.length} tools`);console.log(`${actual}@${initialized.serverInfo.version}: ${tools.length} tools [${tools.join(", ")}]`);return actual}finally{child.kill()}} (async()=>{const actual=[];for(const name of names)actual.push(await test(name));if(new Set(actual).size!==names.length)throw new Error("initialize names are not unique");console.log(`validated ${actual.length} unique MCP server identities`)} )().catch(error=>{console.error(error.message);process.exit(1)});'

<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->
# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

## Motivation
Agent prompts need direct access to the repository's feedback, session, inspection, rule, audit, and compact terminal capabilities. Invalid, incomplete, or identity-colliding workspace MCP registrations prevent GitHub Copilot Chat from discovering those local stdio servers.

## Dependent expectation
If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on GitHub Copilot Chat discovering `feedback-mcp`, `session-mcp`, `peek-mcp`, `rule-mcp`, `audit-mcp`, and `compact-terminal-mcp` after a VS Code window reload. Each server returns its true package name and version in the MCP initialize `serverInfo`, and can enumerate its tools over stdio.

`.vscode/mcp.json` is a repository symlink to `.github/mcp.json`, making the target the single source of truth. The shared target uses only the top-level `servers` object accepted by the VS Code MCP schema. It must not contain the unsupported top-level `mcpServers` compatibility object.

## Guards
- `val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration`: requires editor schema validation, all six registrations, six unique package-level initialize names, and successful MCP `initialize` plus `tools/list` responses.
- Passing evidence: `exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-identities-20260715` includes a live `feedback-mcp` initialize response and its five feedback tools.

## Positions
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .github/mcp.json`: canonical `servers` registrations for the six local MCP packages.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: .vscode/mcp.json`: VS Code-facing symlink to the canonical configuration.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `feedback-mcp` and the package version.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `session-mcp` and the package version.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/peek-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `peek-mcp` and the package version.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `rule-mcp` and the package version.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/audit-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `audit-mcp` and the package version.
- `implemented` — `code_ref: memory-api/tools/mcp/compact-terminal-mcp/src/server.rs`: reports `compact-terminal-mcp` and the package version.

## Governing-rule requirement
The session bootstrap policy at `.rule/rules/89330b3b-4d28-4c48-80dd-203311dbe855/body.md` must introduce task-relevant workspace guidance, including this MCP availability contract when agent tooling configuration is changed.

## Traceability
- Configuration ticket: `.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml`.
- Identity remediation ticket: `memory-api/.ticket/tickets/2318f63f-7113-4987-87b7-ca26afa04d11/ticket.toml`.
- Validation spec: `.test/default/specs/val-vscode-copilot-mcp-registration.json`.
- Passing execution: `.test/default/executions/exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-identities-20260715.json`.

A VS Code window reload is required after registration or server identity changes so Copilot rebuilds its MCP server and tool snapshot.

arguments.title: VS Code Copilot local MCP registration, unique identity, and protocol handshake
arguments.command: cargo test -p feedback-mcp -p session-mcp -p peek-mcp -p rule-mcp -p audit-mcp -p compact-terminal-mcp; build release binaries; launch each binary and require serverInfo.name == package name plus successful tools/list
arguments.detail: Validates schema discovery, package availability, six unique stdio initialize identities, and tool enumeration. feedback-mcp must initialize as feedback-mcp and expose feedback_ingest, feedback_inbox, feedback_query, feedback_mine, and feedback_summary.

data_json.content: The diff is exactly 30 added lines, five per server, with no unrelated edits. I’m now marking all six implementation positions complete and updating the durable validation guard to require unique names and feedback enumeration; then I’ll record the passing execution and advance the ticket.

# VS Code Copilot local MCP server configuration

arguments.description:
## Goal
Make feedback-mcp, session-mcp, peek-mcp, rule-mcp, audit-mcp, and compact-terminal-mcp report their true package identities in MCP initialize responses so VS Code Copilot can distinguish and activate every configured server.

## Confirmed root cause
Each ServerHandler built ServerInfo with `..Default::default()`. rmcp's `Implementation::from_build_env()` is compiled inside the dependency crate, so every consuming server reported the name `rmcp`. Five visible stale processes therefore shared one initialize identity, while feedback-mcp was configured but had no running process and no Copilot launch attempt in the current logs. The identity collision prevented reliable distinction/discovery.

## Implementation
Each of the six ServerHandler implementations now explicitly sets `server_info.name` from `CARGO_PKG_NAME` and `server_info.version` from `CARGO_PKG_VERSION`.

## Validation
- Focused cargo check: passed for all six packages.
- Release build: passed after terminating five stale Windows-locked MCP processes.
- Strict initialize/tools-list handshake: six unique names, correct version 0.1.0, 56 tools total.
- feedback-mcp initialized as `feedback-mcp` and exposed all five feedback tools.
- Focused cargo test: 9 passed across 19 suites.
- Source diagnostics: no errors.
- Focused diff: 30 additions, only explicit server_info metadata.
- `cargo fmt --check` is blocked by unrelated pre-existing feedback-mcp formatting drift; no broad reformat was applied.
- Validation execution: `exec-vscode-copilot-mcp-identities-20260715`.
- Spec: `.spec/specs/1a62c7f7-4f94-420b-9532-3a28b0c1ecd5/spec.toml`.

## Activation
Run `Developer: Reload Window` in VS Code. The stale server processes were stopped and corrected release binaries are ready; Copilot must restart them and rebuild its MCP list.
arguments.author: GitHub Copilot

data_json.content: The new validation evidence is recorded as passed. I’m finalizing the remediation ticket with the confirmed root cause and exact results, then running spec/reference health and source diagnostics once more; after that the only remaining action is the required VS Code reload to let Copilot spawn the corrected binaries, including feedback.

data_json.content:
The root cause was `rmcp`’s default server metadata. Each server used `ServerInfo::default()`, which reported the dependency crate name `rmcp`. That identity collision also prevented reliable discovery of `feedback-mcp`.

Each server now explicitly reports its package name and version:

- `feedback-mcp`
- `session-mcp`
- `peek-mcp`
- `rule-mcp`
- `audit-mcp`
- `compact-terminal-mcp`

Validation passed:

- Six unique MCP initialize identities.
- 56 tools enumerated in total.
- `feedback-mcp@0.1.0` initialized successfully with all five feedback tools.
- Focused tests: 9 passed across 19 suites.
- Corrected release binaries built successfully.

The stale processes were stopped to replace their Windows-locked binaries. Run **Developer: Reload Window** now. Copilot should restart all six servers under their true names and include `feedback-mcp` in the server list.
